_provenance: Mined on 2026-06-10 by read-only recon agents (Cargo.toml dependency reads + source-level signature/verify call-site reads across nucleus + nucleus-platform); checked in verbatim as spike ground truth.

nodes.id: nucleus-receipt (crates.io v1.0.0; on nucleus origin/main, NOT on current checkout branch)

edges.evidence: nucleus-platform/crates/nucleus-auction-hub-server/src/receipt.rs:57 (struct AuctionReceipt) + :178-183 (issuer.inner().signing_key().sign(&canonical), Ed25519) + :91-116 canonical bytes = plain serde_json::to_vec, NOT RFC 8785
nucleus-platform/crates/nucleus-auction-hub-server/Cargo.toml:17; receipt signed by SessionIssuer key, kid mapped to JWKS (src/receipt.rs:64-66)

edges.to: nucleus-receipt (crates.io v1.0.0; on nucleus origin/main, NOT on current checkout branch)
edges.evidence: CLAIMED-BUT-ABSENT: nucleus-platform/crates/nucleus-substrate-core/Cargo.toml:19-39 has NO nucleus-receipt dependency; lib.rs:130 defines its OWN duplicate Receipt; lib.rs:192-198 canonical_signing_bytes uses plain serde_json::to_vec (the non-deterministic-under-preserve_order codec that nucleus-receipt v1.0.0 was created to fix). Re-export = unmerged spiffy PR #129.
ABSENT: hub-server Cargo.toml [dependencies] (lines 15-80) contains no nucleus-substrate-core; the hub never builds a substrate-core Receipt — only its own AuctionReceipt

nucleus/sdks/verifier-js/src/lib.rs:86-90 (verifyBundle parses nucleus_envelope::Bundle, calls verify_bundle defined at nucleus/crates/nucleus-envelope/src/verify.rs:512); fixtures are bundle.json/trust-anchor.json, no AuctionReceipt fixture exists

nucleus/sdks/verifier-js/src/lib.rs:361-364 (recomputeReceipt parses nucleus_recompute::ClearingReceipt and re-derives math); ClearingReceipt (nucleus/crates/nucleus-recompute/src/lib.rs:87-94) is an UNSIGNED tagged enum {Settlement|Commons|Vcg} — no signature_b64/issuer_kid fields, recompute-only, no Ed25519 check

nucleus-platform/crates/nucleus-wasm/src/receipt.rs:1-30 — the ONLY wasm verifier of AuctionReceipt; it RE-IMPLEMENTS canonical bytes ('Rather than drag the HTTP stack into the browser bundle, this module re-implements the exact check') and its byte-identity rests on serde_json non-preserve_order BTreeMap ordering (doc lines 26-30); hub-server is only a dev-dependency for parity tests (Cargo.toml:120, deliberately NOT runtime per :65)

ABSENT — THE CLAIMED EDGE DOES NOT EXIST: grep of AuctionReceipt across the nucleus public repo returns zero hits; verifier-js Cargo.toml (lines 17-57) depends on neither nucleus-receipt, nucleus-substrate-core, nucleus-agent-market, nor any crate defining the hub receipt shape

notes: VERDICT: the claim "a receipt signed by the platform auction hub verifies offline in the open-source WASM verifier SDK" is FALSE as stated. The path has THREE discontinuities. (1) TYPE FORK at the hub: nucleus-auction-hub-server builds its own AuctionReceipt (receipt.rs:57) — {auction, bids, match_result, settlement?} signed by the SessionIssuer Ed25519 key — not nucleus-substrate-core::Receipt and not nucleus-receipt::Receipt; the hub crate depends on neither. (2) BROKEN RE-EXPORT: nucleus-substrate-core does NOT re-export nucleus-receipt from crates.io (no dep in Cargo.toml:19-39); it carries a duplicate Receipt (lib.rs:130) whose canonical bytes are plain serde_json::to_vec (lib.rs:192-198), i.e. it still has the preserve_order signing-bytes hazard that nucleus-receipt v1.0.0 (RFC 8785 JCS, origin/main crates/nucleus-receipt/src/lib.rs:203-210) exists to fix; the re-export is unmerged spiffy PR #129 (blocked on private-org Actions billing). Also note nucleus-receipt is absent from the nucleus checkout's current branch (feat/verified-except-kvm); it lives on origin/main and in the feat/receipt-colimit worktree. (3) VERIFIER MISMATCH: the open-source WASM SDK (sdks/verifier-js, crate nucleus-verifier-wasm, publish=false/unpublished per Cargo.toml:9) signature-verifies nucleus-envelope Bundles (lib.rs:87) and recomputes UNSIGNED nucleus-recompute ClearingReceipts (lib.rs:362-364) — it cannot parse, let alone verify, an AuctionReceipt. The only offline WASM verifier of hub receipts is nucleus-platform/crates/nucleus-wasm — CLOSED source, and it re-implements (not shares) the canonical-bytes routine, with byte-identity explicitly resting on serde_json's default map ordering (nucleus-wasm/src/receipt.rs:26-30 doc). Net: five receipt-ish envelope types coexist (AuctionReceipt, substrate-core::Receipt, nucleus-receipt::Receipt, envelope::Bundle, recompute::ClearingReceipt) with two incompatible canonicalization codecs (plain serde_json vs RFC 8785 JCS); the true offline-verify story for hub receipts today is platform-internal (nucleus-wasm + JWKS), and the public SDK's signature story covers a different artifact entirely.
